title: pinaropappus parvus
intro: Pinaropappus parvus, also known as the Utah pine-squirreltail, is a small shrub-like plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. Some of the health benefits associated with Pinaropappus parvus include relief from arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Pinaropappus parvus can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used topically or internally.
